title: "Kingdom of Days: October 13"
slug: kingdom-of-days-october-13-2
author: Ken Rosen
description: "Happy birthday, Charlie Giordano and The River! Also on this date: Bruce plays an acoustic set at the first Bridge School Benefit, an epic E Street Band performance of “Better Man” with Bruce and Eddie Vedder, and much more."

***New for 2025:** Video for 1980; audio for 2006; new entry for 2024*<br>

**1954:** Future Seeger Sessions Band member and E Streeter Charlie Giordano is born in Brooklyn.<br>

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2021/10/1954-1013.jpg.webp)

**1973:** At the last minute, Columbia Records cancels a promotional preview show for *The Wild, The Innocent & The E Street Shuffle* at the Kennedy Center in Washington, DC. No reason is provided.<br>

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2018/10/1973-1013.jpg.webp)

**1976:** Bruce and the E Street Band bring the Lawsuit Tour to Kean College in Union, New Jersey.<br>

{{< youtube dkDuGnON38Y >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2021/10/medium.jpg.webp)

**1977:** Bruce joins Southside Johnny on stage for four songs (including "[Down in the Valley]({{< relref "2019-01-16-matr-bruce-springsteen-and-southside-johnny-down-in-the-valley.md" >}})" and "[Ain't Too Proud to Beg](../2018-10-07-cover-me-aint-too-proud-to-beg/post.md/)") at The Stone Pony in Asbury Park.<br>

{{< youtube pBaV-gpRnOg >}}

**1980:** Bruce releases his fifth studio album, *The River*, and The River Tour (already in progress) moves on to the St Paul Civic Center Arena for one night. Parts of the show are captured on Super-8 film.<br>

{{< youtube HPMEAOlLFt4 >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2023/10/The_Toronto_Star_1980_10_10_page_59-scaled.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/1980-10-13.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2021/10/1980-10-13a.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/1980-10-13b.png.webp)

**1986:** Neil and Pegi Young hold their first benefit for The Bridge School for children with special needs in Mountain View, California, and Neil enlists a few friends for the inaugural event. Former bandmate Nils Lofgren plays a set (in which Bruce makes a brief guest appearance), and Bruce plays a rare (at the time, at least) acoustic set with Nils and Danny as well. Of note: a new arrangement for "[Seeds]({{< relref "2020-10-10-roll-of-the-dice-seeds.md" >}})" set to the tune of "[Rockaway the Days]({{< relref "2021-03-15-roll-of-the-dice-rockaway-the-days.md" >}})," and an a capella "[You Can Look (But You Better Not Touch)]({{< relref "2020-06-14-roll-of-the-dice-you-can-look-but-you-better-not-touch.md" >}})." Crosby, Stills, Nash & Young join Bruce for "[Hungry Heart]({{< relref "2022-12-21-roll-of-the-dice-hungry-heart.md" >}})," and Tom Petty, Don Henley, and Robin Williams join Bruce and CSNY for the "[Teach Your Children]({{< relref "2018-04-14-matr-crosby-stills-nash-young-and-springsteen-teach-your-children.md" >}})" finale. This show is available as part of Bruce's official archive series–get it [here](https://live.brucespringsteen.net/live-music/0,22746/Bruce-Springsteen---bridge-school-benefit-concert-at-shoreline-amphitheatre-mountain-view-ca-10-13-1986.html).<br>

{{< youtube kiVud8tM5_0 >}}

{{< youtube fHpcfoVQ4eA >}}

{{< youtube bJjXGHKog04 >}}

{{< youtube UsaSHY6PFoM >}}

{{< youtube eCdCbD6iA7g >}}

{{< youtube RjT-Rk-o7zM >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/1986-10-13.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/1986-10-13a.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/1986-10-13b.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/1986-10-13c.png.webp)

**1992:** After an unplanned week off due to illness, Bruce picks World Tour 1992 back up at the Tacoma Dome.<br>

{{< youtube Ja9Ha-Er8BM >}}

{{< youtube iP2ktjDtoO4 >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/1992-10-13.png.webp)

**2004:** Bruce and Jackson Browne hold an encore concert for the Vote for Change Tour at the Continental Airlines Arena in East Rutherford. Patti opens the event, with Bruce joining her for two numbers; Jackson follows, with a Bruce guest-appearance on "[Running on Empty.]({{< relref "2021-03-24-matr-jackson-browne-and-bruce-springsteen-running-on-empty.md" >}})" Bruce's set features guest appearances not only by Jackson Browne (on "[Racing in the Street]({{< relref "2021-10-03-roll-of-the-dice-racing-in-the-street.md" >}})") but notably Eddie Vedder for three numbers featuring Bruce's live debut of "[Better Man]({{< relref "2022-11-10-matr-eddie-vedder-and-bruce-springsteen-better-man.md" >}})" complete with an inspired sax solo by Clarence.<br>

{{< youtube tXL4b44Ncds >}}

{{< youtube D4J54_MvGL0 >}}

{{< youtube iaAK7UTLusA >}}

{{< youtube SnnL8wEDNJM >}}

{{< youtube ZKdlVoUlm8s >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/2004-10-13a.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2021/10/2004-10-13b.png.webp)

**2005:** Bruce brings his solo acoustic Devils & Dust Tour to Chicago's United Center.<br>

{{< videopress id=jjiAlqTY >}}

**2006:** The Seeger Sessions Tour stops in Rotterdam for a show at Sportpaleis Ahoy.<br>

{{< videopress id=80VbpEqs >}}

{{< youtube ATbYBBrMqjU >}}

**2007:** Bruce joins the Max Weinberg 7 for six songs during their benefit performance for the Monmouth County SPCA at Max's farm in Atlantic Highlands, New Jersey.<br>

{{< youtube O8ttwMZYuI4 >}}

{{< youtube go4-ORX38L4 >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/2007-10-13a.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/2007-10-13b.png.webp)

**2009:** Bruce and the E Street Band kick off one final stand of four shows at the Philadelphia Spectrum before its scheduled demolition. Each show will be special in its own right, starting from the first number of the first show: the tour premiere and first E Street Band performance of "[Seaside Bar Song]({{< relref "2022-10-24-roll-of-the-dice-seaside-bar-song.md" >}})" in 36 years. This show also features a full album performance of *Born to Run*, Bruce's only performance of "[Little Bit O'Soul]({{< relref "2019-03-06-cover-me-little-bit-o-soul.md" >}})" to date, and a rare outing for "[The Fever]({{< relref "2020-10-04-roll-of-the-dice-the-fever.md" >}})."<br>

{{< youtube T7CS6kPJ9I8 >}}

{{< youtube qylAN0-IdnM >}}

{{< youtube VL--Tr6CCKM >}}

{{< youtube GClsxQk4q-Y >}}

{{< youtube hlN1x52g9kk >}}

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/2009-10-13a.png.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2020/10/2009-10-13b.png.webp)

**2017:** Bruce settles in for a long run of *Springsteen on Broadway* at the Walter Kerr Theater in New York City. This is Night Two.<br>

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2018/10/2017-1003.jpg.webp)

**2018:** It's Night Two Hundred for *Springsteen on Broadway* at the Walter Kerr Theater in New York City. In attendance to help celebrate: comedian Chris Rock, actor Laurence Fishburne, and author Nelson George.<br>

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/2018-10-13d.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/2018-10-13a.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/2018-10-13b.jpg.webp)

![](https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/lilbud/shuffle-archive/refs/heads/master/assets_webp/2019/10/2018-10-13c.jpg.webp)

**2019:** Bruce spends the weekend at the Ham Yard Hotel in London. speaking to print and broadcast journalists about his *Western Stars* film, most of which will be printed and aired over the weeks to come.<br>

{{< youtube 1qboxvFJ3Jo >}}

{{< youtube wqB6XsBsHbM >}}

**2021:** Without fanfare, Bruce concludes his long-running *From My Home to Yours* radio show with Episode 29, "Let's Talk About Sex."<br>

[https://estreetshuffle.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/2021-10-13-Bruce-E-Street-Radio-DJ-set-29.mp3](https://estreetshuffle.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/2021-10-13-Bruce-E-Street-Radio-DJ-set-29.mp3)
{{< audio url="https://estreetshuffle.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/2021-10-13-Bruce-E-Street-Radio-DJ-set-29.mp3" >}}

**2024:** Bruce performs "The Ghost of Tom Joad" and "Dancing in the Dark" at a benefit gala for the USC Shoah Foundation in New York City.<br>
